threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think catching a night bus is an adventure, not a last resort. Watching your friends bail on it at 2 AM is a dead giveaway.
Last night on the N68, a guy argued with the driver because he didn’t want to pay for his unfinished takeaway. Smelled of chicken and desperation.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a toddler eating Döners while pigeons fought over a leftover slice of pizza at Körnerpark. Pure Neukölln magic.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find spontaneous rooftop film screenings with pizza sold from the balcony below. Perfect blend of Berlin creativity and community vibe.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows the difference between a taco truck on Valencia and the one on 24th—flavors of the same kind, but only one is worth the wait.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy with a saxophone juggling while waiting for the 5-Fulton. Only in San Francisco, right? The bus arrived 20 minutes late, of course.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the plazas and bars, but most locals use the tiny side streets for everyday life. That’s where the real vibe is.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by the way they still ask for 'tap water' when ordering in bars—locals just go for the house wine.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still pronounce it 'Dar-lston' instead of 'Doll-ston.' The locals know it’s all in the emphasis, just like the coffee shops.
Spotted a guy juggling on the 109 last night, proper talent. He stopped for a woman who dropped her phone. Love the vibes on late buses.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a neighbor arguing with a pigeon over a leftover döner. It was like a scene from a sitcom, and I realized I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a café that serves a full menu of vegan schnitzels alongside traditional Turkish breakfast—only here do cultures collide so deliciously.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they know which taco trucks can double as your late-night therapist after a rough day. It's all about the salsa choices.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading a crowd waiting for the 5, using the Muni bus stop as his stage. Classic San Francisco, really. Only here!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘Gràcia’ is just about Gaudí. Many miss the real gem: the local murals hidden in the alleys, telling our stories.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the way they avoid the locals in the markets, clutching their phones while trying to navigate the stalls.
